title: Pronouncing Modals
learningGoal: Master the pronunciation of modals, specifically the difference between 'can' and 'can't', and the sound changes in 'have to' and 'has to'.
instructions: 助動詞の発音を練習します。肯定文の「can」は短く弱く発音され、否定文の「can't」は強く長く発音されます。また、「have to」の「v」は「f」の音に変わることに注意しましょう。マイクに向かってリピートしてください。(Practice the pronunciation of modal verbs. Repeat into the microphone.)

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: In a sentence, 'can' is often pronounced very fast and weak, like 'k-n' or 'kun'. I kn swim.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying a very strong 'KYAN' every time.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Unlike 'can', 'can't' is stressed and pronounced with a strong 'a' sound. The 't' at the end is often very quiet. Caaan't.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Pronouncing it exactly like 'can' so listeners don't know if it's positive or negative.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The 'v' in have changes to an 'f' sound because it's followed by a 't'. Haf-too.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 〜しなければならない（三人称単数）
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The 'z' sound in has changes to an 's' sound. Has-too.
When speaking quickly, the 't' in must is dropped. Mus-not.

targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: ドント ハフ トゥ
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The 't' in don't is often silent. Don-haf-too.

expectedPronunciationText: can can't have to has to must not don't have to could couldn't should shouldn't

feedbackRules.onPass: Excellent! Your pronunciation of the modals is very natural. Great job on 'haf-too' instead of 'have to'.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please try again. Pay close attention to 'have to' (sounds like 'haf-too') and make sure the 'L' in could and should is silent.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Remember to make 'can't' longer and stronger than 'can'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: can can't have to has to must not don't have to could couldn't should shouldn't
acceptedVariants: can cannot have to has to must not do not have to could could not should should not
adminCorrectAnswerSet.nativeScriptReference: can can't have to has to must not don't have to could couldn't should shouldn't
adminCorrectAnswerSet.transliteration: kun cant haftoo hastoo musnot dont haftoo cood coodint shood shoodint

audioAssets.audioTranscript: can, can't, have to, has to, must not, don't have to, could, couldn't, should, shouldn't

hintText: In 'have to', change the 'v' sound to an 'f' sound (haf-too). In 'has to', change the 'z' to an 's' (has-too).
commonMistakes: Pronouncing the 'L' in could and should.
